MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Regarding His Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Thomas W. Jackson of Furth Salem Mason & Li LLP, Tel. no. 1-707-244-9422 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Tom Prountzos of the Law Office of Goodman Neuman Hamilton LLP< Tel. no. 1-415-705-0412 appearing for the Defendant.

Ruling - The matter was argued. The court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT SAM CHING MOTION's Motion For Protective Order Regarding His Deposition And For Sanctions - Motion is denied. Plaintiff is entitled to change defendant's deposition for a total of 3 hours taken in one sitting before 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. Plaintiff to prepare form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro-tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt

Notice And Motion To Amend Pleading To Add Punitive Damages Claim; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-16-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Thomas W. Jackson, Esq. of Furth Salem Mason & Li LLP, T:707.244.9422, for plaintiff/moving party;
Tom Prountzos, Esq. [no card]

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARK DIETRICH's Motion To Amend Pleading To Add Punitive Damages Claim is DENIED. The motion is not timely filed since Mr. Dietrich has not shown that he was unaware of facts necessary to make a CCP 425.13 motion more than nine months prior to July 30, 2018 and he made a diligent effort to obtain any facts he may have needed to make such a motion more than nine months prior to July 30, 2018 as required by Goodstein v. Superior Court (1996) 42 Cal. App. 4th 1635. Nine months prior to July 30, 2018 is October 30, 2017. Prior to October 30, 2017 Mr. Dietrich had filed a complaint including a request for punitive damages and, thus per CCP 128.7(b), his counsel certified that he had a sufficient legal and factual basis to support a request for punitive damages by that date. Mr. Dietrich argument that he had not received the laboratory report or defendants' discovery responses by that date lacks merit. Mr. Dietrich has not shown that he couldn't have with diligence obtained the information in the laboratory report prior to October 30, 2017. Nor has Mr. Dietrich shown that the information in defendants' discovery responses provided him with a quantum of evidence he previously lacked that allowed him to meet the standards of CCP 425.13 which he would not have otherwise been able to meet with the information he did have or reasonably could have had by October 30, 2017.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Set For Trial On Jul-30-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
